prompt: My `ReportGenerator` constructor takes 9 parameters: five repositories, two services, a logger, and a config object. The class is becoming unwieldy. A coworker suggests using a ServiceLocator or a builder to hide the parameters. Is that the right fix?

assertions.text: Rejects ServiceLocator / builder as the right fix (or strongly cautions against)
Reframes the diagnosis: 9 collaborators is an SRP smell, not primarily a DI ergonomics smell
Connects parameter count to actor / responsibility multiplicity (class serves multiple stakeholders)
Correct fix: split ReportGenerator along actor/responsibility lines
Notes that hiding the parameters preserves the underlying problem — symptom suppression, not cure
